basePrompt: electric creature-battle title letters with arcade speed, comet-fang weirdness, chunky sticker energy, sharp readable monster-showdown drama
promptUsed: electric creature-battle title letters with arcade speed, comet-fang weirdness, chunky sticker energy, sharp readable monster-showdown drama Codex learning constraints: Fused-engine diagnosis: clean counters helped, but generic athletic chamfers are not enough. Add oversized creature-specific events: jaw apertures, fang terminals, broken shoulders, hooked counters, and voltage bridges that remain visible at mobile proof size. Prompt patches: Next pass: judge only the undecorated black proof first. Keep the reverse-slanted monster-title stance, but replace repeated esports chamfer chips with one oversized electric-fang structural event per uppercase and numeral. Apply mint fill, heavy black sticker outline, and purple shadow only after the raw silhouettes pass. Mobile hero: larger centered two-line VOLTAGE / FANG with no crop and less dead space. Apply mint fill, black sticker outline, and purple shadow only after the raw silhouettes pass. Lessons: Do not approve Voltage Fang when the decorated hero carries more concept than the raw alphabet. Approval requires mobile-visible, individualized electric-fang silhouettes, especially in C/G/O and 0/6/8/9, plus deliberate mobile scale and tighter proof spacing. Do not approve Voltage Fang when the decorated hero carries the concept better than the raw alphabet. Approval requires visibly individualized electric-fang silhouettes in the black proof, especially C/G/O and 0/6/8/9. Do not approve Voltage Fang while the decorated hero is stronger than the raw alphabet or while mobile crops the title. Approval requires complete mobile composi...
verdict: Passable but not yet approval-grade. The decorated hero has a strong monster-arcade hit, and the mobile crop problem is mostly fixed, but the raw black proof still relies too much on repeated athletic slash/chamfer language. The concept is visible, yet the promised per-glyph fang events are uneven and several key glyphs are not distinctive enough at mobile size.
nextEdits: Proof only the black uppercase and numerals first, without mint, outline, shadow, or gradient.
Redraw C/G/O/0/6/8/9 as a focused group with one large visible body event each, not extra perimeter chips.
Make C an unmistakable open jaw with a lower fang silhouette that changes the aperture shape.
Give G a much larger inward voltage hook that reads before decoration is added.
Make O a closed charged ring with one bold asymmetric break, and make 0 clearly slashed with a different rhythm.
Reduce small repeated chamfer chips by another 30-40% and replace them with fewer, larger structural decisions.
Tighten display spacing for VOLTAGE FANG and CGO AEX while preserving enough room for outlines in the decorated hero.
memoryLesson: Do not approve Voltage Fang just because the hero looks exciting. Approval requires the undecorated black proof to show large, individualized electric-fang construction, especially in C/G/O and 0/6/8/9, with spacing tuned at mobile size.

batch.strategy: Candidate lane: spacing and mobile. Preserve a deliberate VOLTAGE / FANG lockup, reduce descriptor dominance, avoid horizontal clipping, and keep raw proof readable before effects.
